message:
fix(plan): refine D-state precheck to only apply for kill-like actions

Adjust the VerifyProcessState precheck logic to be more precise about
when process state verification is required at apply time.

Previous behavior:
- Added VerifyProcessState precheck for ALL actions targeting D-state processes
- This was overly conservative for Pause actions

New behavior:
- Only add VerifyProcessState precheck for Kill and Restart actions
- Pause/Resume actions skip this precheck for D-state processes

Rationale:
- Kill: Definitely needs state check - D-state processes may ignore SIGKILL
- Restart: Needs state check - involves stopping the process first
- Pause: SIGSTOP may eventually work on D-state processes when I/O completes
- Resume: If process was paused, it's not in D-state anymore

The precheck prevents wasted effort attempting actions that will fail,
while allowing actions that have a reasonable chance of eventual success.

Unit test added: d_state_pause_does_not_require_state_precheck()
- Verifies Pause action on D-state process omits VerifyProcessState

Note: This commit does not yet handle Action::Freeze/Unfreeze variants
in all match statements. Those will be addressed in a follow-up commit
when the freeze action implementation is complete.

Refs: Plan §3.2 (process state awareness), §8 (safety gates)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
